jokes.text: At first I was mad that Jessica Simpson was ahead of me. But then I saw the Dukes of Hazzard. Funny!
jokes.setup: Jenna being upset about magazine ranking
jokes.punchline: Praising terrible movie as 'funny' when clearly jealous
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna - transparently fake praise hiding jealousy. Well-constructed ironic delivery.

jokes.text: Actually, they use salad dressing, 'cause it gets a better sheen.
jokes.setup: Question about what looks like baby oil
jokes.punchline: Casual expertise about photography lighting tricks
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jenna's casual knowledge of modeling tricks reveals her desperation and experience.

jokes.text: Ya burnt!
jokes.setup: Liz questioning the comedy relevance
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate use of Dave Chappelle catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag of Jenna misusing 'Ya burnt!' - character-specific misappropriation of pop culture.

jokes.text: I'm laughin', I'm horny, let's do this!
jokes.setup: Defending Maxim shoot as career move
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely honest assessment of magazine's appeal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Jenna's shameless pursuit of attention. Economical and character-defining.

jokes.text: Well, you look happy. Somebody just have a root beer float?
jokes.setup: Jack looking pleased
jokes.punchline: Childish explanation for adult businessman's happiness
jokes.explanation: Liz's tendency to infantilize situations - treating Jack like a child who needs simple pleasures.

jokes.text: Can't do that. Make it 4:00 AM. / That's no good. / 10:00. / Stop insulting me. / 3:00 AM. / Midnight. / You bring the coffee. / 2:30, you bring the coffee. / That's my final. / Done.
jokes.setup: Jack wanting to practice negotiation
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards escalation getting earlier and more demanding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that subverts negotiation logic - getting MORE unreasonable while 'compromising'.

jokes.text: Right, I think you mean mano a toddler.
jokes.setup: Jack saying 'mano a mano with Josh'
jokes.punchline: Liz correcting to emphasize Josh's childishness
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that serves character development - Liz protecting Josh while being witty.

jokes.text: I would like to be Michelle Pfeiffer to your angry black kid who learns that poetry is just another way to rap.
jokes.setup: Jack wanting to teach Liz about negotiation
jokes.punchline: Overly specific reference to inspirational teacher movie tropes
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - pretentious reference combined with casual racism, delivered with complete confidence.

jokes.text: These sunglasses have a chip in them that makes the lenses change color as my iPod loses power.
jokes.setup: Talking about young women buying anything
jokes.punchline: Completely useless but technologically complex product
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd product that satirizes both tech gadgets and consumer culture.

jokes.text: That's not how you play marbles, Jack. / But that's how you keep them.
jokes.setup: Jack's metaphor about hiding marbles
jokes.punchline: Liz corrects game rules, Jack doubles down on corruption
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Liz's literal honesty vs Jack's casual corruption.

jokes.text: Yo, remember that email we got from those Nigerians that needed our help gettin' all that money out of Africa? We did it! I got the check today.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning Nigerian email scam
jokes.punchline: Believing he actually helped and got money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - his wealth making him gullible to obvious scams.

jokes.text: Want to go to Vegas and buy a bunch of sarcophagi? / Nah. I don't even use the ones I have.
jokes.setup: Tracy suggesting Vegas celebration
jokes.punchline: Dotcom casually admitting to owning unused sarcophagi
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - both the suggestion and the casual rejection reveal wealth-based insanity.

jokes.text: Fatballz? / Studying hotel administration at Cornell. / Well, go ahead, Fatballz! That's a good program.
jokes.setup: Looking for entourage members with ridiculous names
jokes.punchline: Respectful approval of education despite absurd nickname
jokes.explanation: Juxtaposes ridiculous street name with genuine pride in education - unexpectedly sweet.

jokes.text: I love this cornbread so much I wanna take it behind a middle school and get it pregnant.
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing love for cornbread
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate metaphor for affection
jokes.explanation: Memorably disturbing metaphor that perfectly captures Tracy's unfiltered, inappropriate expressions of joy.

jokes.text: The sun'll come out Tomorrow... Fa la la la
jokes.setup: Kenneth auditioning to harmonize with Tracy
jokes.punchline: Visual of grown men singing Annie together badly
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of awkward male bonding through show tunes - mildly amusing setup for relationship.

jokes.text: Where's Jackée?
jokes.setup: Looking at 'sexiest women in comedy' magazine
jokes.punchline: Tracy's outdated reference to 80s sitcom actress
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy - pop culture reference that's both specific and slightly off-target for the context.

jokes.text: 'Cause I tried to mention Bono as much as possible.
jokes.setup: Asking if she came across as interesting
jokes.punchline: Bizarre strategy for appearing intellectual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna logic - shallow understanding of how to appear deep or worldly.

jokes.text: but it's just a bunch of gay guys that like to get in silly costumes and prance around.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming she didn't say she hates the troops
jokes.punchline: Audio of her saying exactly that (about theater)
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding comedy - Jenna was talking about theater, reporter heard military.

jokes.text: These Hollyweirdos from La La Land like Jenna Moron-ey are just un-American!
jokes.setup: TV outrage about Jenna's comments
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing her name as 'Moron-ey'
jokes.explanation: Straightforward pun on her name - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: and their parent company the Sheinhardt Wig Corporation.
jokes.setup: Boycotting NBC and General Electric
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous parent company name
jokes.explanation: Establishes the show's fictional corporate structure with absurdist touch - wigs as media empire.

jokes.text: Should I prepare a song?
jokes.setup: Being told she needs to apologize on Hardball
jokes.punchline: Thinking a song would be appropriate for political show
jokes.explanation: Pure Jenna - everything is a performance opportunity, even damage control.

jokes.text: Does my vulva look swollen? Come here.
jokes.setup: Josh approaching for friendly conversation
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that fits character but relies on discomfort rather than wit.

jokes.text: Could I get free Choco Sticks?
jokes.setup: Learning advertisers love him
jokes.punchline: Immediate focus on free snacks rather than leverage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Josh - childlike priorities despite being in power position.

jokes.text: We've got some serious troubs, my friend. We are not smiles-times.
jokes.setup: Agent talking about contract problems
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Hollywood agent speak
jokes.explanation: Great parody of agent-speak - made-up phrases that sound like they should mean something.

jokes.text: What your skedge mañana? 'Cause this is gonna be one serious negosh.
jokes.setup: Agent continuing conversation
jokes.punchline: More abbreviated Hollywood speak
jokes.explanation: Continues the agent parody - 'skedge' and 'negosh' are perfect fake abbreviations.

jokes.text: I love Halo so much, I wanna take it out behind a middle school and get it pregnant!
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing love for video game
jokes.punchline: Using the same inappropriate metaphor from earlier
jokes.explanation: Direct callback - less original but establishes Tracy's consistent inappropriate expressions.

jokes.text: I like to keep things fresh so much, that I wanna take it out behind the middle school and get it pregnant.
jokes.setup: Kenneth pointing out repeated joke
jokes.punchline: Tracy immediately using it again while claiming freshness
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy - Tracy using the joke while claiming to avoid repetition. Self-aware escalation.

jokes.text: You could say that you support the troops, but you feel that the war was poorly planned and started under false pretenses. And that we should have used those resources to hunt down Osama Bin Laden.
jokes.setup: Coaching Jenna for political TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Liz giving her own political views as coaching
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment - Liz can't help injecting her own politics into damage control.

jokes.text: Obama, what is he, Hispanic? / No, he's black. / And he's running for President? / Good luck.
jokes.setup: Jenna being told to support Obama
jokes.punchline: Jenna's ignorant and casually racist assumptions
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but character-accurate - Jenna's privileged ignorance about politics and race.

jokes.text: All right, let's skip the foreplay and get right to the penetraish.
jokes.setup: Agent starting negotiation
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor with made-up agent word
jokes.explanation: Continues agent characterization with inappropriate sexual metaphor and fake vocabulary.

jokes.text: I mean, Yaznach is coming up.
jokes.setup: Listing Jewish holidays Josh wants off
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up Jewish holiday
jokes.explanation: Absurdist touch - fake holiday that sounds plausibly Jewish to non-Jewish ears.

jokes.text: One dollar.
jokes.setup: Agent listing reasonable demands
jokes.punchline: Jack's insultingly low counteroffer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - maximum insult with minimum words. Pure power move.

jokes.text: These photos I found of Josh roughhousing with Lance Bass at SeaWorld.
jokes.setup: Jack producing leverage
jokes.punchline: Completely innocent activity treated as scandalous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - 'roughhousing with Lance Bass at SeaWorld' is perfectly innocent yet sounds compromising.

jokes.text: My offer is now 75 cents.
jokes.setup: Josh defending the photos as innocent
jokes.punchline: Jack making offer even worse
jokes.explanation: Escalates the power play - Jack getting crueler as he gains advantage.

jokes.text: Awse.
jokes.setup: Agent realizing they have leverage
jokes.punchline: Another made-up agent exclamation
jokes.explanation: Continues the agent speak - 'awesome' becomes 'awse' in abbreviated Hollywood talk.

jokes.text: I knew you weren't ready for a big chair.
jokes.setup: Jack angry at Liz for betraying information
jokes.punchline: Treating her like child who can't sit at adult table
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack condescension - treating professional colleague like misbehaving child.

jokes.text: You're marrying my mom, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Grizz and Dotcom saying they need to talk
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's bizarre assumption about serious conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - completely left-field assumption that reveals his unusual worldview.

jokes.text: If Kenneth can beat me, and you can beat Kenneth, then by the transitive property, you should beat me too!
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to figure out gaming hierarchy
jokes.punchline: Applying math logic to video game skills
jokes.explanation: Clever use of actual mathematical concept in gaming context - Tracy showing unexpected intelligence.

jokes.text: What is the world's only egg-laying mammal? / The Easter bunny! / Right again!
jokes.setup: Grizz testing Tracy's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong answer treated as correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of letting Tracy win - absurd wrong answer enthusiastically validated.

jokes.text: You know, for someone who's super super hot, you're really cranky.
jokes.setup: Tucker Carlson attacking her
jokes.punchline: Jenna's inappropriate response to political criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna - turning political debate into personal attractiveness discussion.

jokes.text: why doesn't he hunt down and capture Barrack Obama before he strikes again?
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to make political point
jokes.punchline: Confusing Obama with Osama bin Laden
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup - Jenna's political ignorance leads to predictable but painful mistake.

jokes.text: Ya burnt!
jokes.setup: Jenna thinking she won the political debate
jokes.punchline: Misusing the catchphrase after embarrassing herself
jokes.explanation: Callback to running gag - Jenna using 'Ya burnt' inappropriately when she's actually lost.

jokes.text: I have written an op ed piece for the New York Times under Jenna's name in which I put the media on trial.
jokes.setup: Jack handling the Jenna situation
jokes.punchline: Completely ghostwriting sophisticated political commentary for shallow actress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack solution - overly intellectual response to simple PR problem.

jokes.text: He told me he was a pallbearer at his brother's wedding.
jokes.setup: Defending Josh's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Revealing his confusion of wedding/funeral roles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Josh characterization through Liz's inadvertent revelation - mixing up funeral/wedding terminology.

jokes.text: what happened in your childhood to make you believe that people are good?
jokes.setup: Liz defending Josh and choosing friendship
jokes.punchline: Jack treating basic human decency as psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character line - Jack's worldview where cynicism is normal and kindness is pathological.

jokes.text: Also, that Oscar you have is made of chocolate. And that lady you European kissed last night was actually a gentleman...
jokes.setup: Kenneth deciding to tell Tracy the truth
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing revelations about Tracy's life
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - each revelation gets more uncomfortable, building to Tracy cutting him off.

jokes.text: Josh? You stupid turd.
jokes.setup: Liz discovering Josh interviewing for other shows
jokes.punchline: Uncharacteristically harsh insult from usually nice Liz
jokes.explanation: Character break - Liz's anger making her use uncharacteristically juvenile insult.

jokes.text: You were opening for a puppet when I found you.
jokes.setup: Liz angry about Josh's betrayal
jokes.punchline: Revealing Josh's embarrassing past career low point
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail - 'opening for a puppet' is perfectly humiliating backstory.

jokes.text: what kind of moron calls out sick and then comes to work to have a meeting? / I get an NBC discount here.
jokes.setup: Liz pointing out Josh's obvious mistake
jokes.punchline: Josh's petty reason for the location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Josh logic - choosing restaurant discount over maintaining his deception.

jokes.text: Well, he wanted Dwayne Jr.'s number, so I gave it to him. / You did what? / Damn it, K, now I have to hear from Tanisha's mom on how they both mad at the twins.
jokes.setup: Kenneth handling Tracy's entourage business
jokes.punchline: Complex web of fictional character relationships
jokes.explanation: Absurdist world-building - creates complex fictional social network around Tracy.

jokes.text: Television on! / Pornography!
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to turn on TV by voice command
jokes.punchline: TV randomly turning to porn channel
jokes.explanation: Visual gag - Tracy's technological incompetence leading to embarrassing result.

jokes.text: I called my friend Saul Sheinhardt at our parent company Sheinhardt Wigs who called his nephew Morty Sheinhardt who called his son, John Stewart.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining how he killed Josh's Daily Show opportunity
jokes.punchline: Absurd family connection chain to Jon Stewart
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of corporate conspiracy - makes Jon Stewart part of wig company family business.

jokes.text: Ya burnt!
jokes.setup: Jack successfully crushing Josh
jokes.punchline: Jack appropriating Jenna's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Callback that shows Jack picking up the office's casual language - momentarily unprofessional.

jokes.text: I have a really bad sex addiction.
jokes.setup: Josh explaining why he needs the money
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing in professional meeting
jokes.explanation: Josh's lack of social boundaries - treating contract negotiation like therapy session.

jokes.text: Do the worm! / Good Lord, the worm! That's so degrading. Are its origins German?
jokes.setup: Liz making Jack humiliate Josh
jokes.punchline: Jack's simultaneous disgust and intellectual curiosity about dance move
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - even disgusted by lowbrow dance, he can't help analyzing its cultural origins.

jokes.text: Okay, the pinwheels are lit, but they're not spinning. When they're not spinning, they look exactly like... Swastikas.
jokes.setup: Patriotic TV special with pinwheel props
jokes.punchline: Visual disaster creating Nazi imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy disaster - patriotic show accidentally creating worst possible imagery.

jokes.text: I love you so much, I'm gonna take you behind the middle school and get you pregnant.
jokes.setup: Tracy reconciling with his entourage
jokes.punchline: Using the inappropriate metaphor for genuine affection
jokes.explanation: Final callback to running gag - now used for genuine emotional moment, making it oddly sweet.

jokes.text: I love you so much, I'm gonna take you behind the middle school and get you pregnant.
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing love for his friends
jokes.punchline: Final use of the inappropriate metaphor
jokes.explanation: Running gag conclusion but diminishing returns on repetition
